senators.name: Richard Blumenthal
senators.nerdReason: Harvard graduate (chair of The Harvard Crimson), studied at Trinity College Cambridge, J.D. from Yale Law School where he was editor-in-chief of the Yale Law Journal. Two of the most prestigious editorial positions in American academia. Served 20 years as Connecticut Attorney General, demonstrating sustained rigorous legal and analytical expertise. Marine Corps Reserve veteran.
education: J.D., Yale Law School (Editor-in-Chief, Yale Law Journal)
B.A., Harvard University (Chair, The Harvard Crimson)

senators.name: Chris Murphy
senators.nerdReason: Dual B.A. in history and political science from Williams College with a year at Oxford, J.D. from UConn. Known as one of the Senate's most thoughtful and research-driven policy voices. Deep expertise in gun violence prevention (became national leader after Sandy Hook), foreign policy, and healthcare. Published author. Brings intellectual rigor and evidence-based advocacy to every policy area he touches.
education: J.D., University of Connecticut School of Law
B.A. History and Political Science, Williams College

CT-01.name: John Larson
CT-01.notNerdReason: B.A. from Central Connecticut State University. Senior Fellow at Yale Bush Center in Child Development. Career as teacher, insurance agency owner, and state legislator. Long-tenured in Congress but approach is more dealmaker than analytical. Social Security reform focus shows policy commitment but not data-driven methodology.
education: Senior Fellow, Yale Bush Center in Child Development and Social Policy
experience: President Pro Tempore, Connecticut State Senate
High School History Teacher and Athletics Coach

CT-02.name: Joe Courtney
CT-02.notNerdReason: B.A. from Tufts University and J.D. from UConn School of Law. Career as public defender and state legislator. Deep expertise in defense and submarine policy as ranking member on Seapower subcommittee. Solid policy knowledge but more legislative craftsman than analytical policy wonk.
education: J.D., University of Connecticut School of Law
experience: Ranking Member, Armed Services Seapower Subcommittee

CT-03.name: Rosa DeLauro
CT-03.nerdReason: B.A. from Marymount College, M.A. from Columbia University, and M.A. from London School of Economics. Two master's degrees from world-class institutions. 30+ years in Congress with deep expertise in appropriations, public health, and labor policy. Ranking Member of House Appropriations Committee. Known for meticulous, data-driven oversight of federal spending and health policy.
experience: Chief of Staff to Sen. Christopher Dodd

CT-04.name: Jim Himes
CT-04.nerdReason: B.A. in social studies from Harvard and M.Phil. in philosophy from Oxford as a Rhodes Scholar. 12-year career at Goldman Sachs rising to Vice President, heading the telecommunications technology group. Ranking Member of the House Intelligence Committee. Combines elite academic credentials, quantitative financial expertise, and deep intelligence/national security analytical rigor. One of the most intellectually credentialed members of the House.
education: M.Phil. Philosophy, University of Oxford (Rhodes Scholar)

CT-05.name: Jahana Hayes
CT-05.nerdReason: M.A. in curriculum and instruction from University of Saint Joseph, built on degrees from Southern CT State and Naugatuck Valley CC. Named 2016 National Teacher of the Year — the highest recognition of teaching excellence in the U.S., requiring demonstrated innovation and analytical rigor in education. 15 years teaching history with evidence-based pedagogical methods. Now applies education expertise on the House Education and Workforce Committee.
education: M.A. Curriculum and Instruction, University of Saint Joseph
experience: History Teacher, John F. Kennedy High School, 15 years
